sections.title: Act of a child under seven years of age
sections.summary: Nothing is an offence which is done by a child under seven years of age.
sections.example: If a 6-year-old child accidentally fires a gun, they cannot be criminally prosecuted.

sections.title: Things done in private defence
sections.summary: Nothing is an offence which is done in the exercise of the right of private defence.
sections.example: If someone attacks you with a knife and you hit them back to save your life, it is not a crime.

sections.title: Murder
sections.summary: Culpable homicide is murder if the act is done with the intention of causing death.
sections.example: Intentionally poisoning someone's drink to kill them is murder.

sections.title: Punishment for voluntarily causing hurt
sections.summary: Punishes anyone who voluntarily causes bodily pain, disease, or infirmity to another.
sections.example: Slapping someone during a heated argument can lead to charges under this section.

sections.title: Assault or criminal force to woman with intent to outrage her modesty
sections.summary: Protects women from physical assault intended to violate their dignity.
sections.example: Tugging at a woman's clothes or making unwanted physical contact is a serious offence.

sections.title: Theft
sections.summary: Moving movable property out of the possession of any person without their consent.
sections.example: Taking a laptop from a friend's house without asking them is theft.

sections.title: Criminal breach of trust
sections.summary: Dishonestly misappropriating property that was entrusted to you.
sections.example: An employee using company funds for personal vacations is a breach of trust.
